name: Gliffy Diagramming Tool API
what_it_does: Online diagramming tool with REST API — enabling teams to create flowcharts, wireframes, UML, network, and org chart diagrams, deeply integrated with Atlassian Confluence and Jira as embedded diagrams, with drag-and-drop editor, version history, and shape libraries for technical documentation.
use_cases: Creating and embedding Gliffy diagrams directly in Confluence pages via Atlassian Marketplace app
Managing Gliffy diagrams programmatically via REST API for organizational workflows
Exporting Gliffy diagrams as PNG, SVG, or JPEG via API for documentation pipelines
Integrating Gliffy with Jira issues for visual architecture and workflow documentation
Querying organization diagram inventory for governance and documentation audits
not_for: High-fidelity UI/UX design (use Figma for pixel-perfect interface design)
Real-time collaborative whiteboarding (use Miro for open-ended brainstorming)
Complex prototyping with animations (use Proto.io or Axure for rich interactions)
best_when: An Atlassian-centric team (Confluence + Jira) needs embedded diagramming without leaving their documentation environment — especially for technical architecture diagrams and process flows in Confluence.
avoid_when: You're not using Confluence or Jira, need advanced whiteboarding, or your team is established with Lucidchart, Draw.io, or Miro.

auth.notes: API key from Gliffy account settings. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. No webhooks. Atlassian Marketplace app for Confluence and Jira Cloud. REST API for diagram management. No OAuth2. Primary integration pathway is Atlassian ecosystem, not direct REST API. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.paid_tiers: Team plans from $7.99/user/month; Confluence Cloud integration priced per Atlassian tier
pricing.notes: San Francisco company. Founded 2005. Acquired by Perforce Software (2019). Perforce also owns Hansoft, Helix Core, and other dev tools. Gliffy's primary value is Confluence/Jira integration via Atlassian Marketplace. Significant competition from Draw.io (free), Lucidchart, and Miro. 8M+ users. Gliffy Diagram for Confluence is the flagship product — standalone is secondary. Post-Perforce acquisition, focus has shifted to Atlassian ecosystem depth.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for diagram management. API key bearer auth. Limited public API documentation. No webhooks. Primary integration is via Atlassian Marketplace apps (Confluence/Jira), not direct REST API. Perforce-owned. Rate limits not documented. API is secondary to the Atlassian plugin integration pathway.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Diagram reads fully idempotent. Diagram creation not idempotent — check by name before creating. Export operations idempotent by diagram ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Primary value is Atlassian Marketplace integration (Confluence/Jira) — not standalone REST API
Limited public API documentation — primary integration is Atlassian plugin, not REST API
No webhooks — must poll for diagram change events
Perforce acquisition may affect product direction and API investment
Draw.io (free, open source) provides similar Confluence integration — evaluate cost vs. Draw.io

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US data residency. API key auth. Perforce-owned company with enterprise security standards. Diagram content may contain confidential system architectures — restrict Confluence page permissions. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes for API access. Atlassian Marketplace integration inherits Atlassian's security model.
